title: Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread Loaf

seo.title: Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread Recipe | Fluffy & Easy Loaf
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread at home! This recipe uses the tangzhong method for an incredibly soft, fluffy loaf every time.
longTailKeywords: how to make Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread
easy Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread recipe
Shokupan Milk Bread recipe step by step
best homemade Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread
seo.h1: The Best Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread Loaf Recipe
h2Headings: What is Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread?
Step-by-Step Instructions for Japanese Milk Bread
Pro Tips for the Best Shokupan Results
seo.imageAlt: A freshly baked loaf of Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread, golden brown and perfectly risen, on a cooling rack.
seo.imageCaption: Bake your own incredibly soft and fluffy Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread loaf at home with this easy recipe!

description: Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread is renowned for its incredibly soft, fluffy texture and delicate sweetness. Utilizing the tangzhong method, this loaf achieves an unparalleled moist crumb that stays fresh for days. Perfect for sandwiches, toast, or simply enjoyed on its own, it's a staple in Japanese baking.

instructions.title: Prepare the Tangzhong
instructions.text: In a small saucepan, whisk together 25g bread flour and 125ml water until smooth. Cook over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens to a paste-like consistency, resembling wallpaper paste (about 2-3 minutes). Do not boil. Remove from heat, transfer to a small bowl, cover with plastic wrap directly on the surface to prevent a skin from forming, and let it cool completely to room temperature. This can be done in advance.

instructions.title: Combine Dry Ingredients
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl or the bowl of a stand mixer, combine the remaining 275g bread flour, granulated sugar, instant dry yeast, and salt. Whisk briefly to ensure all dry ingredients are evenly distributed.

instructions.title: Mix Wet Ingredients and Tangzhong
instructions.text: In a separate small bowl, whisk together the warm milk and the large egg. Once the tangzhong has cooled, add it to the wet ingredient mixture and whisk until well combined.

instructions.title: Form the Dough
instructions.text: Pour the wet ingredient mixture (including the tangzhong) into the bowl with the dry ingredients. Mix with a spatula or the dough hook of a stand mixer on low speed until a shaggy dough forms. Continue mixing for 5-7 minutes until the dough comes together.

instructions.title: Add Butter and Knead
instructions.text: Add the softened unsalted butter to the dough. Continue kneading on medium speed for another 10-15 minutes, or until the dough is smooth, elastic, and passes the windowpane test (you can stretch a small piece thinly enough to see light through it without tearing).

instructions.title: First Proof
instructions.text: Form the dough into a ball and place it in a lightly oiled bowl. Cover with plastic wrap or a damp towel and let it rise in a warm place for 1 to 1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Shape the Loaf
instructions.text: Gently punch down the dough to release air. Transfer it to a lightly floured surface. Divide the dough into three equal pieces. Roll each piece into a flat oval, then fold the top third down and the bottom third up. Roll each piece into a tight log. Place the three logs side-by-side in a greased 9x5 inch loaf pan.

instructions.title: Second Proof
instructions.text: Cover the loaf pan with plastic wrap or a damp towel and let it rise in a warm place for another 45-60 minutes, or until the dough has risen about 1 inch above the rim of the pan.

instructions.title: Bake the Bread
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 190°C (375°F). Once preheated and the dough has proofed, bake for 30-35 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the internal temperature reaches 90-95°C (195-205°F). If the top browns too quickly, you can loosely tent it with foil.

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the loaf from the oven and immediately transfer it to a wire rack to cool completely. This prevents the bottom from becoming soggy. Slice and serve once fully cooled for the best texture.

tips: Ensure your tangzhong is completely cooled before adding it to the rest of the ingredients; hot tangzhong can kill the yeast.
Use high-protein bread flour for the best results, as it develops strong gluten for a chewier, more elastic crumb.
For extra softness, brush the top of the hot loaf with a little melted butter immediately after removing it from the oven.
article: Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread Loaf is a beloved Japanese Baking dish enjoyed across Japanese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
